Vinculum

Autonomous software delivery on Kubernetes

DocsSource

Collective orchestration for real repositories

Turn requirements into reviewed, tested, and deployed software.

Vinculum combines drone workers, a Kubernetes operator, built-in identity, a Forgejo control surface, and deployable Helm charts into one cluster-native platform.

Read the docsOpen Vinculum Code
Runtime surfaces
  • Hive UIControl surface for drones, repositories, tasks, and reviews.
  • Vinculum CodeForgejo-based code forge with OIDC and project automation.
  • IdentityKeycloak realm used for browser auth and service integration.

Platform

Purpose-built for agent swarms, not single-shot demos.

Autonomous Delivery Loops

Coder, reviewer, tester, and deployer drones work through requirements, implementation, review, and release as durable Kubernetes workloads.

Cluster-Native Control Plane

The orchestrator owns CRDs, schedules drone jobs, tracks task state, and exposes an API and Hive UI for operational visibility.

Built-In Platform Stack

Forgejo, Keycloak, PostgreSQL, bootstrap automation, and Helm packaging ship together so a clean cluster can become a working development platform quickly.

Workflow

One platform, four persistent layers.

1. Requirements and planning

Requirements become durable resources instead of chat history, so the operator can schedule, review, and revisit work later.

2. Drone execution

Workers run in isolated pods with mounted instructions, auth, and repository access scoped through Kubernetes objects.

3. Platform bootstrap

Vinculum Infra reconciles Keycloak and Forgejo so identity, organizations, and OIDC wiring come up with the stack.

4. Ship and operate

Helm charts, GHCR images, and cluster ingress make the stack installable, reproducible, and externally reachable.

Docs

Start with the operational model.

Deployment Model

Publishing flow, GHCR images, OCI Helm charts, and GitHub Actions behavior.

Operational Reset

Cluster reset, reinstall expectations, ingress, and Cloudflare tunnel placement.

Architecture Guide
